problems.label: 1a
problems.group: 1. Evaluate the expressions when the value of the variable is given.

problems.label: 2a
problems.group: 2. Evaluate 100 − x² for the given values of x.

problems.label: 3a
problems.group: 3. Find the value of the expressions if p = 14 and s = 5.

problems.label: 4a
problems.group: 4. Answer these questions about the expressions in problem 3.
problems.display: Which of expressions 3a, 3b, 3c, and 3d had the same value?

problems.label: 4b
problems.display: Check if those same expressions still have the same value using other values for p and s.

problems.label: 4c
problems.display: Do those expressions always have the same value? (equivalent expressions)

problems.label: 5a
problems.group: 5. Write a single expression for each problem, then find the value.
problems.display: Total value in cents: five 10¢ coins, five 5¢ coins, two 20¢ coins, seven 50¢ coins

problems.label: 5b
problems.display: Margaret has 64 marbles. Tom has 15 less. Henry has twice Tom's amount. How many does Henry have?

problems.label: 5c
problems.display: What is the area of the coloured part? (see workbook p. 48, rectangle diagram)

problems.label: 5d
problems.display: What is the area of the coloured part? (see workbook p. 48, square diagram)
